danger_signals.visual_form: Military/police uniforms and weaponry, authoritarian visual design
danger_signals.meaning: Threat of state violence and forced capture; danger to protagonist

danger_signals.visual_form: Implied through UI/environment—all-powerful computational presence controlling Union City
danger_signals.meaning: Omnipresent surveillance and control threat; systemic danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Harsh environmental design, barren landscape, isolation
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; survival challenge; danger from nature and isolation
Death, loss, catastrophe; catalyst for danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Tribal gathering, shelter, group presence in wilderness
safety_signals.meaning: Sanctuary; protection; social safety and belonging

safety_signals.visual_form: Companion AI with changeable body/shell, personality-driven interactions
safety_signals.meaning: Ally; emotional support; technological aid and humor counterbalance to threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Inventory items, puzzle solutions, crafted objects
safety_signals.meaning: Agency and competence; ability to survive and solve problems

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: LINC (Logical Inter-Neural Connection) computer system as symbolic center
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armed security soldiers, authoritarian aesthetics, centralized control architecture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban city-state, polluted cyberpunk infrastructure, hierarchical institutional design

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Economic competition markers, 'cheap, gimmicky garbage' products flooding market
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sabotage evidence, market-driven chaos vs. state control, competing power center
Wilderness, tribal shelter, natural/organic aesthetic, survival-based culture

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Dystopian future; Earth damaged by pollution/nuclear fallout
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cyberpunk aesthetic; polluted cityscapes; harsh wasteland environments; deteriorated infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Survival necessity; authoritarian control as response to chaos; technological dependency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Centralized state power (LINC); labor representation removed; rigid institutional hierarchy
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Government kidnaps citizens without explanation; forced relocation; state violence normalized

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Economic war between Union City and Hobart Corporation
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Market saturation imagery; competitive sabotage traces; contrasting market aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Corporate/state espionage; dialogue referencing economic dominance struggle; market manipulation

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Helicopter crash / protagonist separated from tribe
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wreckage, destruction, isolation, transition from wilderness to urban confinement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Foster kidnapped; tribe annihilated; protagonist forced into city; loss of agency

environmental_storytelling.example: Wordless/narrative comic illustrating Foster's childhood crash, Aboriginal adoption, and survival—establishes worldview before gameplay begins
The Gap (Outback) as harsh but community-rich; Union City as technologically advanced but authoritarian—visual geography reflects political ideology
Foster's Lager can found at crash site becomes his name origin—mundane object carries narrative weight and world history
Characters remark on Hobart Corporation's market dominance and sabotage—dialogue conveys economic conflict and propaganda uncertainty
Joey's personality-driven remarks on his changing 'shells'—humanized technology reflects world where consciousness/identity is transferable and fragmented

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Orders kidnapping without explanation; omnipresent surveillance; authoritarian decision-making
Armed aggression; eliminate witnesses (tribal members); abduct citizens forcibly
Escapes captivity; investigates systemic corruption; uses engineering skills to solve problems and survive
Provides technical aid, commentary, and emotional balance; transfers between bodies; personality preserved across forms
Nurture and educate Foster; teach wilderness survival; foster technological innovation; killed by security forces

notes: Beneath a Steel Sky uses visual and narrative contrasts to communicate world-state: dystopian cyberpunk urban landscape (danger, control, pollution) vs. harsh but communal wilderness (danger, autonomy, belonging). Factions are distinguished by ideological markers (state neodemocracy vs. corporate competition vs. indigenous community) rather than explicit color/symbol systems. Danger signals emanate from institutional power (LINC, security forces, authoritarian control) and environmental hazards. Safety emerges from community bonds and individual competence. Political situation (economic war, labor suppression, state violence) is conveyed through environmental design, NPC dialogue, and plot structure. The comic prologue and inventory-based storytelling embed world-state information into core gameplay mechanics.
